text.title: Figma AI Ecosystem Boundary Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the figma-ai-ecosystem methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Figma Make / Draw / Sites / Image Tools MUST be classified as UI-only surfaces; agents cannot drive them.
rule.rationale: Public Figma docs confirm no agent API for these features.

rule.statement: Agent role MUST be prompt preparation, validation, or post-publish audit — never UI puppetry.
rule.rationale: UI puppetry breaks silently when Figma ships UI changes.

rule.statement: Automation MUST target REST API + Webhooks for agent-driven flows.
rule.rationale: These are the only documented + supported agent surfaces.

rule.statement: Hybrid workflows MUST document the handoff point (agent → designer) explicitly with a 'commit' gate.
rule.rationale: Implicit handoffs create finger-pointing when output is wrong.

rule.statement: Boundary decision MUST be revisited quarterly because Figma ships AI surface changes every Config conference.
rule.rationale: A stale boundary leaves automation on the table.

rule.statement: When preconditions hold, run the full procedure end-to-end and emit the output artefact.
rule.rationale: Routes positive decision-tree leaves to the procedure.

rule.statement: When preconditions fail or skip-conditions hold, exit without producing the artefact and document why.
rule.rationale: Routes negative decision-tree leaves out without polluting the output channel.
